name: Zero-Knowledge Patterns
title: Verifiable Tool Execution Patterns (ZK)
description: Patterns for proving correct execution of agent actions without revealing sensitive internal states or raw data.
anti_patterns: Using low-entropy salts that are susceptible to rainbow table attacks.
Hardcoding salt values in publicly readable knowledge artifacts.
Neglecting to verify tool checksums before signing an execution proof.

axiomAlignment.A1_verifiability: ZK patterns provide mathematical or logical proof of correctness.
axiomAlignment.A4_non_harm: Prevents data leakage by ensuring only the minimum necessary proof is shared.

operation_commitment.description: Agents commit to a specific input/output hash before and after execution.

implementation.proof: A signed receipt verifying that the transition from pre to post followed the authorized logic.

tokenized_execution.description: Sensitive data is replaced with tokens before being passed to a tool; the agent only proof-checks the result.
tokenized_execution.use_case: Processing PII or financial data via external MCP servers.

did_decoupled_audit.description: Actions are attributed to ephemeral DIDs to prevent direct profile correlation while maintaining accountability.
did_decoupled_audit.implementation: Generate a session-specific DID sub-key for tool interactions.

best_practices: Use salt in hashes to prevent rainbow table attacks on low-entropy inputs.
Verify tool integrity using checksums before execution.
Keep sensitive salt values in L0 protected memory.
